Text.reasoning:
**Document scan for source text indicators:**
Title hierarchy reveals "HAMLET / DE SHAKESPEARE" → clear attribution pattern ● Shakespeare = playwright identifier, not translation credit here.

Translation marker present: "Traduction : Yves Bonnefoy" but this is adapter/director credit, not original creation ● Original work predates any French adaptation by centuries.

**Ontological boundary check:**
This is definitively NOT an adaptation of existing French material — no mention of prior translator or different source language variants ○ The phrase structure suggests direct English-to-French mapping rather than intermediate step.

**Core extraction logic:**
"HAMLET" = play title ● "DE SHAKESPEARE" = creator attribution following standard French program convention ("de" indicating authorship) ● No publication details visible so cannot create Publication entity reference ◐

→ `_label`: combines both elements into single descriptive string covering full bibliographic context
→ `identified_by` array with Name object containing Title classification + normalized Getty AAT term 
→ `created_by` links to Person entity representing William Shakespeare specifically through Creation activity type
